intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Crucifera tristis". However, I found information on the plant "Crucifera" or more specifically the genus "Crucifera" is actually the Brassicaceae family. 

Assuming you're referring to the Brassicaceae family, which includes plants like broccoli, cauliflower and cabbage, here's a paragraph about the plant genus Brassica.

The Brassicaceae family, also known as Crucifera, is a genus of flowering plants that includes a wide variety of species such as broccoli, cauliflower, and cabbage. The health benefits of these plants include their high antioxidant content, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation, as well as their potential to support cardiovascular health and improve immune function. The bioactive constituents of Brassica plants include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Brassica plants include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
